description_long: This object is a karambit-style knife featuring a dramatically curved, single-edged blade with a sharp point, designed for slashing and hooking motions. The blade is metallic and has a sleek, dark finish, giving it a modern and tactical appearance. The handle is ergonomically contoured with finger grooves for a secure grip and includes a prominent ring at the end, typical of karambit knives, allowing for versatile handling and retention. The handle appears to be made of a durable synthetic or polymer material, contrasting slightly with the metallic blade. There are no visible decorative elements or engravings, emphasizing a utilitarian and functional design. The overall construction suggests a compact, lightweight tool suitable for close-quarters use.
description: A sleek, curved karambit knife with a dark metallic blade and an ergonomic handle featuring a finger ring. The design is modern, functional, and lacks ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curve of the blade, ergonomic handle, and finger ring.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's curve, handle contours, and finger ring.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the blade's spine, emphasizing the blade's sharpness and handle thickness.
attribute_handle_design: contoured black polymer with finger grooves and a circular finger ring
